Video Collaboration, Gaming, Pointing Devices and Tablet &amp; Other Accessories, partially offset by sales declines in Mobile Speakers and Smart Home.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Asia Pacific</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The increase in sales in fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> compared with fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> was primarily driven by sales increases in Video Collaboration, Gaming, Keyboards and Combos and PC Webcams, partially offset by sales declines in Mobile Speakers, Audio PC &amp; Wearables and Tablet and Other Accessories. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The increase in sales in fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> of 17% compared with fiscal year 2018 was primarily driven by sales increases in Video Collaboration, Keyboard &amp; Combos and Pointing Devices, offset by sales declines in Mobile Speakers.</span></div><div><br/></div><div><div style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Creativity &amp; Productivity market:</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Pointing Devices</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our Pointing Devices category comprises PC- and Mac-related mice including trackballs touchpads and presenters. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Pointing Devices sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">1%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily driven by the increases in sales of cordless mice, partially offset by a decline in sales of our corded mice and presentation tools.  </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Pointing Devices sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">4%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily driven by the increases in sales of cordless mice and presentation tools. The increase in cordless mice was led by strong contribution from MX family of premium cordless mice, including the Vertical Wireless Mouse introduced in the second quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> as well as continued performance for MX Master 2S Wireless Mouse and B220 Silent Mouse. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Keyboards&#160;&amp; Combos</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our Keyboards&#160;&amp; Combos category comprises PC keyboards, living room keyboards and keyboard/mice combo products.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Keyboards&#160;&amp; Combos sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">7%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily driven by an increase in sales of our cordless and corded keyboards and wireless keyboard/mice combos, partially offset by a decline in sales of our living room keyboard products. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Keyboards&#160;&amp; Combos sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">8%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily driven by an increase in sales of wireless keyboard/mice combos, mainly from increased sales of our MK 540, MK270 and MK110 wireless combo, and an increase in sales of wireless PC keyboards. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">PC Webcams</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our PC Webcams category comprises PC-based webcams targeted primarily at consumers.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, PC Webcams sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">7%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily driven by an increase in sales of our Webcam C260, Logitech StreamCam launched in AMR and EMEA in the fourth quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, and C270i iPTV CAM, partially offset by a decline in sales of our Webcam C170.  </span></div><div><br/></div><div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Logitech International S.A. | Fiscal 2020 Form 10-K | 50</span></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"></hr><div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br/></span></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><a style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;" href="#sF1610ED6CFCE58BD88DB8DC99BF37527"><span style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Table of Contents</span></a></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br/></span></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br/></span></div></div><div><br/></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, PC Webcams sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">8%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily driven by the increases in sales of our HD Pro Webcam C920 and 1080 Pro Stream Webcam. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Tablet&#160;&amp; Other Accessories</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our Tablet&#160;&amp; Other Accessories category primarily comprises keyboards for tablets.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Tablet&#160;&amp; Other Accessories sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">5%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily driven by the introduction of our Slim Folio Pro for the 11" and 12.9" iPad Pro introduced in the first quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and sales of our Rugged Combo and Slim Folio for a newer generation of iPads introduced in the third quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, partially offset by a decline in sales of our Slim Folio for the 2017/2018 iPad and Slim Combos Keyboard cases for 10.5" iPad. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Tablet&#160;&amp; Other Accessories sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">19%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily driven by the introduction of our Slim Folio keyboard cases, education-based Rugged Combo 2, Slim Combo keyboard cases, Crayon (a digital pencil) and POWERED (a wireless charging dock for iPhone) during fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Gaming market:</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Gaming</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our Gaming category comprises gaming mice, keyboards, headsets, gamepads, steering wheels, simulation controllers, console gaming headsets, console gaming controllers, and Streamlabs services.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Gaming sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">6%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily driven by an increase in sales of our gaming mice, gaming keyboards, console gaming controllers, and gaming steering wheels, and sales from Streamlabs services as a result of our business combination (see Note 3 to the consolidated financial statements). The growth was partially offset by a decline in sales of our gaming headsets and console gaming headsets. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Gaming sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">32%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily driven by an increase in sales of our core PC gaming products, and ASTRO console gaming headsets, which benefited from the growing gaming market, growth in eSports, expansions in new channels and regions, and expansion in product portfolios. The increase for fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> was also driven by the fact that the acquisition of ASTRO closed on August 11, 2017, in the middle of our fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> second quarter, resulting in a partial comparative period impact. The growth was partially offset by a slight decline in our simulation products. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Video Collaboration market:</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Video Collaboration</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our Video Collaboration category includes Logitech&#8217;s ConferenceCams, which combine affordable enterprise-quality audio and high definition (HD) 1080p video to bring video conferencing to businesses of any size.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Video Collaboration sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">41%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily due to increases in sales of our Rally Ultra-HD PTZ Conference Camera, our Tap Touch Controller introduced in the first quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and our MeetUp video conference camera, partially offset by a decrease in sales of older generation products. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Video Collaboration sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">42%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily due to an increase in sales for our MeetUp and PTZ Pro 2 video conference cameras, our BRIO Pro Webcam, our C925E Webcam, and the introductions of our Rally and Rally Ultra-HD PTZ Conference Camera in the third quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. </span></div><div style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">.&#160;The decrease was primarily driven by declines in sales of our existing Ultimate Ears speakers. The decrease was partially offset by sales from the introductions of our Ultimate Ears MEGABOOM 3 and BOOM 3 mobile speakers in the second quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Audio&#160;&amp; Wearables</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our Audio &amp; Wearables category comprises PC speakers, PC headsets, in-ear headphones, premium wireless audio wearables and studio-quality microphones for professionals and consumers.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Audio &amp; Wearables sales decreased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">1%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The decrease was primarily driven by declines in sales of our PC Speakers and Jaybird traditional wireless products, partially offset by the growth in sales of our Blue Microphones products, as a result of our business combination in the second quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, and Jaybird True wireless products. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Audio &amp; Wearables sales increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">10%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase&#160;was primarily driven by an increase in sales of our corded headsets and sales from Blue Microphones products as a result of our business combination (see Note 3 to the consolidated financial statements), partially offset by a decrease in sales of our PC speakers and Jaybird wireless in-ear headphones.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Smart Home market:</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;">Smart Home </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our Smart Home category mainly comprises our Harmony line of advanced home entertainment controllers and home security cameras.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Smart Home sales decreased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">12%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The decrease was primarily driven by an overall decline in sales in our Harmony remote products, partially offset by an increase in sales of our home security products. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, Smart Home sales decreased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">45%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The decrease was primarily due to a decline in sales of our Harmony remotes and home security cameras. </span></div><div><a id="sE6F16D08524F5FDB9CAF2CC93E951427"></a></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Gross style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Gross profit consists of sales, less cost of goods sold (which includes materials, direct labor and related overhead costs, costs of manufacturing facilities, royalties, costs of purchasing components from outside suppliers, distribution costs, warranty costs, customer support costs, shipping and handling costs, outside processing costs and write-down of inventories), amortization of intangible assets and purchase accounting effect on inventory.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Gross margin </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">increased</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> by </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">50</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> basis points to </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">37.7%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> during fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">increase</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> in gross margin was primarily driven by favorable product mix and benefits from cost savings and operational efficiencies, partially offset by unfavorable currency exchange rates, an increase in U.S.-China tariffs and COVID-19 related costs primarily due to disruptions and higher costs in our manufacturing, supply chain and logistics operations and outsourced services.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Gross margin </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">increased</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> by </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">180</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> basis points to </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">37.2%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> during fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">increase</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> in gross margin was primarily driven by favorable product mix and cost reductions. In addition, extra costs incurred due to the transition of the distribution center in North America in the third quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> negatively affected the gross margin in fiscal year 2018. </span></div><div><br/></div><div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Logitech International S.A. | Fiscal 2020 Form 10-K | 52</span></div></div><hr </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span Selling</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Marketing and selling expenses consist of personnel and related overhead costs, corporate and product marketing, promotions, advertising, trade shows, technical support for customer experiences and facilities costs.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span selling expenses increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$45.1 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to&#160;fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily driven by an increase of $36.6 million in personnel-related costs due to increased headcount, partially resulting from the Blue Microphones and Streamlabs acquisitions and increased performance-based variable compensation and an increase of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$7.2 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> in third-party costs. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During&#160;fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, marketing and selling expenses increased $52.8 million, compared to&#160;fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase was primarily driven by an increase of $25.4 million in third-party costs including advertising and marketing expenses to support our new products and an increase of $23.9 million in personnel-related costs due to increased headcount, partly due to the Blue Microphones Acquisition and increased performance-based variable compensation.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Research and Development</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Research and development expenses consist of personnel and related overhead costs for contractors and outside consultants, supplies and materials, equipment depreciation and facilities costs, all associated with the design and development of new products and enhancements of existing products.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year&#160;</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, research and development expenses increased </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$16.4 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to&#160;fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The&#160;decrease was primarily driven by a decrease of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$8.8 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> in personnel-related costs, partially offset by an increase of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$2.4 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> in infrastructure costs.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During&#160;fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, general and administrative expenses increased $2.4 million, compared to&#160;fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The&#160;increase was primarily due to an increase of $2.8 million in personnel-related costs and an increase of $1.4 million in third-party costs including consulting costs, partially offset by a decrease of $1.9 million in infrastructure costs.&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><span style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Amortization of intangible assets consists of amortization of acquired intangible assets, including customer relationships and trademarks and trade names. Acquisition-related costs include legal expense, due diligence costs, and other professional costs incurred for business acquisitions.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The increase in amortization of intangible assets and acquisition-related costs from fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> to </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> was primarily driven by the intangible assets acquired through the Blue Microphones acquisition in the second quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and the Streamlabs Acquisition in the third quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase in amortization of intangible assets from fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> to </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> was primarily due to the Blue Microphones Acquisition in fiscal year 2019 and the ASTRO Acquisition in fiscal year 2018. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Change in Fair Value of Contingent Consideration for Business Acquisition</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"><br/></span></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The change in fair value of contingent consideration resulted from the growth in Streamlabs&#8217; net sales since our acquisition and revised projected net sales in the remaining earn-out period.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The change in fair value of contingent consideration for business acquisition during fiscal year 2018 was primarily due to lower-than-expected sales of Jaybird products, and revised projected sales of Jaybird products during the remaining Jaybird Acquisition earn-out period, primarily driven by supply constraints, an evolving product portfolio and changes in the competitive target market. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">In October 2017, Logitech and the sellers of Jaybird entered into an agreement fully, irrevocably and unconditionally releasing Logitech from the earn-out rights and payments in exchange for $5.0 million in cash.</span></div><div style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During the first quarter of fiscal year 2019, we implemented a restructuring plan to streamline and realign our overall organizational structure and reallocate resources to support long-term growth opportunities. In July 2018, the Board of Directors approved additional costs under this restructuring plan, totaling pre-tax charges of approximately $10.0 million to $15.0 million, of which </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;text-align:right;vertical-align:bottom;">11.4 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> has been recognized cumulatively as of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">March&#160;31, 2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The total charges consisted of cash severance and other personnel costs and are presented as restructuring charges (credits), net in the Consolidated Statements of Operations. As of June&#160;30, 2019, the Company had substantially completed this restructuring plan. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The restructuring-related activities for the year ended March 31, 2018 include activities from our restructuring plan implemented in fiscal year 2016.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><span style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Investment income (loss) related to the style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Currency exchange loss, net relates to balances denominated in currencies other than the functional currency in our subsidiaries, as well as to the sale of currencies, and to gains or losses recognized on currency exchange forward contracts. We do not speculate in currency positions, but we are alert to opportunities to maximize currency exchange gains and minimize currency exchange losses.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Gain on investments, net, represents the realized gain on sales of investment, and unrealized gain (loss) from the fair value change on the available-for-sale securities and equity-method investments during the periods presented. On March 2, 2020, we sold our </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$5.5 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> investment in a privately held company for a total proceeds of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$45.3 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> consisting of (i) </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$3.0 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> in cash, of which </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$0.8 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> is held in escrow, (ii) a </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">6%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> subordinated note with a principal amount of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$8.4 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> due in 5 years together with the interest, at a fair value of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$7.4 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, and (iii) </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">33.9 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> Series A preferred units and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">33.9 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> Series B common units in Marlin-SL Topco, LP ("Marlin"), representing an ownership interest of approximately </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">11.8%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, with a face value of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$33.9 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and fair value of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$35.0 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, respectively. As a result, we recognized a gain of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$39.8 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2020.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The components of net periodic benefit cost other than the service cost component for the years ended </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">March&#160;31, 2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> are included in the line &#8220;Other&#8221; above as a result of adopting ASU 2017-07 effective April 1, 2018. The impact to the comparative period was immaterial and therefore the prior period statements of operations were not revised.</span></div><div><a id="s985B874315BD5700A4C60FABB8E5EA9C"></a></div><div style="vertical-align:bottom;padding-left:2px;padding-top:2px;padding-bottom:2px;padding-right:2px;"><div style="overflow:hidden;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#160;</span></div></td><td colspan="2" style="vertical-align:bottom;padding-left:2px;padding-top:2px;padding-bottom:2px;"><div style="text-align:right;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">10.2</span></div></td><td style="vertical-align:bottom;padding-right:2px;padding-top:2px;padding-bottom:2px;"><div style="text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">%</span></div></td></tr></table></div></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The change in the effective income tax rate between fiscal years </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> was primarily due to the mix of income and losses in the various tax jurisdictions in which we operate and the income tax accounting impact from the enactment of TRAF in the canton of Vaud in Switzerland on March 10, 2020 to take effect as of January 1, 2020.  We have benefited from a longstanding tax ruling from the canton of Vaud through December 31, 2019.  We reached an agreement with the Vaud Tax Administration that would allow for a tax step-up of goodwill to be amortized over ten years beginning on January 1, 2020 as a transition measure. We recorded an income tax benefit of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$151.7 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, net of unrecognized tax benefits to account for the book and tax basis difference of the step-up upon enactment.  The deferred income tax benefit from other temporary differences resulting from the Swiss tax reform, net of three-month amortization of the tax step-up amounted to </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$1.5 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The aggregate deferred income tax impact in fiscal year 2020 as a result of the enactment of TRAF was </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$153.2 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">.</span></div><div><br/></div><div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Logitech International S.A. | Fiscal 2020 Form 10-K | 56</span></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"></hr><div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br/></span></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><a style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;" href="#sF1610ED6CFCE58BD88DB8DC99BF37527"><span style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">Table of Contents</span></a></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br/></span></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br/></span></div></div><div><br/></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">In addition, we recognized excess tax benefits from share-based payments, net of shortfalls of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$6.4 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$10.1 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> in the United States and recognized income tax benefit from the reversal of uncertain tax positions from the expiration of statutes of limitations in the amount of $4.0 million and $2.9 million in fiscal years 2020 and 2019, respectively.  </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The change in the effective income tax rate between fiscal years </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> was primarily due to the mix of income and losses in the various tax jurisdictions in which we operate and provisional income tax accounting impact from the enactment of H.R.1, also known as the "Tax Cuts and Jobs Act" (the Tax Act) in the United States on December 22, 2017. The Tax Act reduced the corporate income tax rate in the United States from 35% to 21%. We recorded a provisional income tax charge of $21.7 million, net of valuation allowance against tax credits, in fiscal year 2018 to remeasure the deferred tax effects at 21%. Furthermore, we recognized $10.1 million and $13.4 million of excess tax benefits from share-based payments, net of shortfalls, in fiscal years 2019 and 2018, respectively. In the same periods, there were tax benefits of $2.9 million and $8.3 million, respectively, from the reversal of uncertain tax positions from the expiration of statutes of limitations. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">As of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">March&#160;31, 2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, the total amounts of unrecognized tax benefits due to uncertain tax positions were $140.8 million and $76.5 million, respectively, all of which would affect the effective income tax rates if recognized.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">As of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">March&#160;31, 2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, we had $40.8 million and $36.4 million, respectively, in non-current income taxes payable, including interest and penalties, related to our income tax liability for uncertain tax positions. We recognized $2.0 million, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$0.6 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and $0.6 million in interest and penalties related to unrecognized tax positions in income tax expense during fiscal years </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, respectively. As of March&#160;31, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, we had $4.5 million and $2.5 million, respectively, of accrued interest and penalties related to uncertain tax positions.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">We file Swiss and foreign tax returns. We received final tax assessments in Switzerland through fiscal year 2017. For other foreign jurisdictions such as the United States, we are generally not subject to tax examinations for years prior to fiscal year 2017. We are under examination and have received assessment notices in foreign tax jurisdictions. If the examinations are resolved unfavorably, there is a possibility that they may have a material negative impact on our results of operations.</span></div><div><a id="s4BF23A1BB52C5C8BA45BFD381DB243A2"></a></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Liquidity and Capital Resources</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Cash Balances, Available Borrowings, and Capital Resources</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">As of March&#160;31, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, we had cash and cash equivalents of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$715.6 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared with </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$604.5 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> as of March&#160;31, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. Our cash and cash equivalents consist of bank demand deposits and short-term time deposits, of which </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">75%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> is held in Switzerland and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">11%</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> is held in China (including Hong Kong). We do not expect to incur any material adverse tax impact, except for what has been recognized, or be significantly inhibited by any country in which we do business from the repatriation of funds to Switzerland, our home domicile.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">As of March&#160;31, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, our working capital was </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$700.3 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">,&#160;compared with working capital of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$632.6 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> as of March&#160;31, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase in working capital over fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> was primarily due to higher balances of cash and cash equivalents, higher accounts receivable, net, and lower balance of accounts payable, partially offset by lower inventories and higher accrued and other liabilities.  </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">We had several uncommitted, unsecured bank lines of credit aggregating to </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$81.4 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> as of March&#160;31, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. There are no financial covenants under these lines of credit with which we must comply. As of March&#160;31, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, we had outstanding bank guarantees of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$20.7 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> under these lines of credit. </span></div><div><br/></div><div><div goods sold for the most recent quarter.</span></div></td></tr></table><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;"><tr><td style="width:48px;"></td><td></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:top"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;padding-left:24px;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">ITO is determined using ending inventories and the annualized cost of goods sold (based on cost of goods sold for the most recent quarter).</span></div></td></tr></table><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">DSO as of March 31, 2020 decreased by&#160;5&#160;days to&#160;</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">50</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;days, as compared to&#160;55&#160;days as of March 31, 2019, primarily due to timing of customer payments and sales linearity. DSO as of March 31, 2019 increased by&#160;22&#160;days to&#160;55&#160;days, as compared to&#160;33&#160;days as of March 31, 2018. The adoption of Topic 606 negatively impacted our DSO for the&#160;year ended March 31, 2019 by&#160;18 days, mainly as a result of changes in the balance sheet presentation of certain reserve balances previously shown net within accounts receivable which are now presented as accrued and other current liabilities. The adoption of Topic 606 did not have an impact over the total cash flows from operating, investing or financing activities. Timing of sales also increased DSO by four days as of March 31, 2019 relative to the corresponding prior year period.  </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">DPO as of March&#160;31,&#160;2020 decreased 11 days, compared to March&#160;31,&#160;2019, primarily due to less inventory replenishment during the fourth quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> due to COVID-19 impact and an increase in cost of goods sold due to higher sales growth. DPO as of March&#160;31,&#160;2019 decreased  five days, compared to March&#160;31,&#160;2018, primarily due to the timing of purchases. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">ITO as of March&#160;31, 2020 was higher compared to March&#160;31, 2019, primarily due to higher sales growth and lower replenishment in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2020 due to COVID-19 impact. ITO as of March&#160;31, 2019 was lower compared to March&#160;31, 2018, due to higher inventory related to new product introductions and inventory from the Blue Microphones Acquisition. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">If we are not successful in launching and phasing in our new products launched during the current fiscal year, or we are not able to sell the new products at the prices planned, it could have a material impact on our revenue, gross profit margin, operating results including operating cash flow, and inventory turnover in the future.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, we generated $</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">425.0 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> in cash from operating activities. Our main sources of operating cash flows were from net income, after deducting the gain on sale of investment in a privately held company, adding back non-cash expenses of depreciation, amortization, share-based compensation expense, change in fair value of contingent considerations, and from changes in operating assets and liabilities. The increase in accounts receivable, net was primarily driven by growth and timing of sales.</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;color:#ff0000;">&#160;</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The decrease in accounts payable was primarily driven by the timing of purchases and inventory supply constraint in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2020. The decrease in inventories was primarily driven by sales growth and less inventory purchases during the fourth quarter of fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The increase in accrued and other liabilities was primarily due to higher accrued personnel expenses.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Net cash used in investing activities was </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$130.2 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, primarily due to </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$91.6 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> of the purchase price (net of cash acquired) for business acquisitions and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$39.5 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> of purchases of property, plant, and equipment. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Net cash used in financing activities was </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$176.7 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, primarily due to </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$124.2 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> of cash dividends paid during the year, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$50.4 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> of repurchases of our registered shares and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$24.3 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> of tax withholdings related </span></div><div><br/></div><div><div Contents</span></a></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br/></span></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br/></span></div></div><div><br/></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">to net share settlements of restricted stock units, partially offset by </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$22.2 million</span><span style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our expenditures for property, plant and equipment during fiscal year&#160;</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> were primarily for tooling and equipment, and computer hardware and software. Our expenditures for property, plant and equipment during fiscal years&#160;</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;and&#160;</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;were primarily for tooling and equipment, computer hardware and software and leasehold improvements. Our expenditures for property, plant and equipment increased during fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, compared to fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, primarily due to higher computer and software and leasehold improvements. Our expenditures for property, plant and equipment decreased during fiscal year 2019, compared to fiscal year 2018, primarily due to a lower amount of tooling purchases. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our payments for acquisitions, net of cash acquired, during fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> were primarily for the Streamlabs Acquisition. Our payments for acquisitions, net of cash acquired, during fiscal year 2019 were primarily for the Blue Microphones Acquisition (refer to "Note 3 - Business Acquisitions" to the consolidated financial statements). Our payments for acquisitions, net of cash acquired, during fiscal year 2018, were primarily for the ASTRO Acquisition.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The purchases and sales of trading investments during fiscal years </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> represent mutual fund activity directed by participants in a deferred compensation plan offered by one of our subsidiaries. The mutual funds are held by a Rabbi Trust.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">During fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, there was a </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$7.1 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> loss from effect of currency exchange rate changes on cash and cash equivalents, compared to a loss of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$10.1 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> from effect of currency exchange rate changes during fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, and a </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$4.7 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> gain of currency translation exchange rate effect during fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. The loss from effect of currency rate changes during fiscal year </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> were primarily due to the weakening of the Euro, Brazilian real and Australian dollar versus the U.S. Dollar by 3%, 25%, and 14%, respectively. The loss from the effect of currency exchange rate changes during fiscal year 2019 were primarily due to the weakening of Euro and Chinese Renminbi versus the U.S. Dollar by 9% and 7%, respectively.</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Cash Outlook</span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our principal sources of liquidity are our cash and cash equivalents, cash flow generated from operations and, to a much lesser extent, capital markets and borrowings. Our future working capital requirements and capital expenditures may increase to support investments in product innovations and growth opportunities or to acquire or invest in complementary businesses, products, services, and technologies. The future impact of COVID-19 cannot be predicted with certainty and may increase our costs of capital and otherwise adversely affect our business, results of operations, financial conditions and liquidity. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">In May 2020, the Board of Directors recommended that we pay cash dividends for fiscal year 2020 of CHF </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">134.0 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> (</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$138.7 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> based on the exchange rate on March&#160;31, 2020). In fiscal year 2020, we paid a cash dividend of CHF </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">121.8 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> (U.S. Dollar amount of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$124.2 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">) out of fiscal year 2019 retained earnings. In fiscal year 2019, we paid a cash dividend of CHF </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">110.7 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> (U.S. Dollar amount of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$114.0 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">) out of fiscal year 2018 retained earnings. In fiscal year 2018, we paid a cash dividend of CHF </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">100.0 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> (U.S. Dollar amount of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$104.2 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">) out of fiscal year 2017 retained earnings. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">In May 2020, our Board of Directors approved a new share buyback program, which authorizes us to invest up to </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$250.0 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> to purchase our own shares, following the expiration date of the 2017 share buyback program. Although we enter into trading plans for systematic repurchases (e.g. 10b5-1 trading plans) from time to time, our&#160;share buyback program provides us with the opportunity to make opportunistic repurchases during periods of favorable market conditions and is expected to remain in effect for a period of three years. Shares may be repurchased from time to time on the open market, through block trades or otherwise. Opportunistic&#160;purchases may be started or stopped at any time without prior notice depending on market conditions and other factors. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-indent:30px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">In March 2017, our Board of Directors approved the 2017 share buyback program, which authorized us to invest up to </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$250.0 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> to purchase our own shares. As of&#160;March&#160;31, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, the remaining amount that may be repurchased under the program is </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$137.4 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">. This program expired at the end of April 2020, and there was no share buyback subsequent to March 31, 2020. </span></div><div style="line-height:120%;padding-bottom:10px;padding-top:10px;text-align:left;text-indent:32px;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">For over ten years, we have generated positive cash flows from our operating activities, including cash from operations of </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$425.0 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">$305.2 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;text-align:right;vertical-align:bottom;">$346.3 million</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"> during fiscal years </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2020</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2019</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, and </span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">2018</span><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">, respectively. If we do not generate sufficient operating cash flows to support our operations and future planned cash requirements, our operations could be harmed and our access to credit facilities could be restricted or eliminated. </span></div><div><br/></div><div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><span style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Logitech International S.A.
